metadata.source: Historical archives, Government of India Gazettes, corporate histories, and economic surveys of India
metadata.description: Comprehensive dataset of Indian actors driving technical change. Batch 1 of 5.
metadata.introduction: A granular view of the dynamics structuring technical change in India. This batch covers foundational state apparatus, key research institutes, premier educational institutions, and strategic industrial entities. Events detail the evolution from post-independence nation-building and the Green Revolution to the IT boom and the 'Make in India' era.

actors.name: Department of Science and Technology (DST)
actors.description: Key government body for promoting scientific research and innovation.

events.description: Established under the Ministry of Science and Technology.
Launched the 'Science and Technology Policy 2003' to double R&D investment.

actors.name: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)
actors.description: National space agency driving satellite launch and remote sensing technology.

events.description: Established under the Department of Atomic Energy.
Successfully placed Rohini satellite in orbit using SLV-3.
Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) entered Mars orbit, first Asian nation to succeed.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the lunar south pole.

actors.name: Indian Institutes of Technology (IIT System)
actors.description: Premier autonomous public technical universities, backbone of India's engineering prowess.

events.description: IIT Kharagpur inaugurated as the first IIT.
Institutes of Technology Act declared them 'Institutions of National Importance'.
IIT Roorkee converted to an IIT.

actors.name: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)
actors.description: Umbrella organization for 37 national laboratories.

actors.name: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS)
actors.description: Largest Indian IT services company, pioneer of the software export industry.

events.description: Established as a division of Tata Sons.
Secured first international project for Interbank Transfer System.
Became first Indian company to cross $1B annual revenue in IT services.

actors.name: Reliance Industries Limited (RIL)
actors.description: Major player in energy, petrochemicals, and telecommunications (Jio).

events.description: Founded as Reliance Textiles Engineers Pvt Ltd.
Commissioned the world's largest refinery complex in Jamnagar.
Launched Reliance Jio, triggering a 4G data revolution in India.

actors.name: Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
actors.description: Agency responsible for military research and development.

events.description: Formed by merger of Technical Development Establishment and Directorate of Technical Development.
Tested the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile.

actors.name: Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)
actors.description: Apex body for coordinating agricultural education and research.

events.description: Established as Imperial Council of Agricultural Research.
Played pivotal role in launching the Green Revolution (Wheat varieties).

actors.name: Infosys Limited
actors.description: Global leader in next-generation digital services.

events.description: Founded by N.R. Narayana Murthy and six others in Pune.
Became the first Indian company to list on NASDAQ.
Launched 'Infosys Aikido' for AI and automation services.

actors.name: Department of Atomic Energy (DAE)
actors.description: Governs nuclear energy research and applications.

events.description: Established under the direct charge of the Prime Minister.

actors.name: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC)
actors.description: India's premier nuclear research facility.

events.description: Established as Atomic Energy Establishment, Trombay.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Science (IISc)
actors.description: Premier institute for advanced scientific and technological research.

events.description: Established by Jamsetji Tata and the Mysore government.
Ranked as India's top university in QS rankings.

actors.name: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL)
actors.description: Largest engineering and manufacturing enterprise in India's energy sector.

events.description: Merged with Bharat Heavy Plates & Vessels.

actors.name: Wipro Limited
actors.description: Leading global information technology, consulting and business process services company.

events.description: Founded as Western India Vegetable Products Limited.
Entered the IT industry under Azim Premji.
Launched Wipro Holmes AI and automation platform.

actors.name: National Informatics Centre (NIC)
actors.description: Premier science and technology organization for informatics services.

events.description: Established under the Department of Electronics.
Launched NICNET, the satellite-based computer network.

actors.name: Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI)
actors.description: Created Aadhaar, the world's largest biometric ID system.

events.description: Issued the first Aadhaar number to a resident of Maharashtra.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Madras (IITM)
actors.description: Top-ranked engineering institute known for deep-tech startups.

events.description: Inaugurated with technical assistance from West Germany.
Incubated Agnikul Cosmos, a private space startup.

actors.name: Larsen & Toubro (L&T)
actors.description: Major Indian multinational engaged in technology, engineering, construction.

events.description: Founded by Henning Holck-Larsen and Soren Kristian Toubro.
Built the indigenous nuclear steam generator for Kakrapar plant.

actors.name: HCL Technologies
actors.description: Pioneer in modern computing and IT services.

events.description: Designed and produced the first indigenous microcomputer.

actors.name: National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI)
actors.description: Umbrella organization for retail payments and settlement systems.

events.description: Founded with support from RBI and IBA.
Launched Unified Payments Interface (UPI), revolutionizing digital payments.

actors.name: Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR)
actors.description: Apex body for formulation, coordination and promotion of biomedical research.

events.description: Established as Indian Research Fund Association.
Validated first indigenous COVID-19 RT-PCR kit.

actors.name: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC)
actors.description: Largest government-owned oil and gas explorer.

events.description: Incorporated under the Oil and Natural Gas Commission Act.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (IITB)
actors.description: Leading institute known for computer science and engineering.

events.description: Established with assistance from UNESCO and Soviet Union.

actors.name: Maruti Suzuki India Limited
actors.description: Largest automobile manufacturer in India, catalyst for auto component industry.

events.description: Launched the Maruti 800, modernizing Indian roads.
Turned into a private limited company under Suzuki Motor Corporation.

actors.name: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL)
actors.description: State-owned aerospace and defense electronics company.

events.description: Delivered Akash missile systems to Indian Air Force.

actors.name: Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL)
actors.description: Government-owned steel making company.

events.description: Established as Hindustan Steel Private Limited.

actors.name: Biocon Limited
actors.description: India's largest biopharmaceutical company.

events.description: Founded by Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw as an enzyme manufacturing company.
IPO oversubscribed 33 times, marking biotech arrival in India.

actors.name: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL)
actors.description: State-owned aerospace and defence company.

events.description: Incorporated as Hindustan Aircraft Limited by Walchand Hirachand.
Taken over by the Government of India.
First successful flight of Light Combat Helicopter.

actors.name: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC)
actors.description: Premier R&D organization for IT, electronics, and supercomputing.

events.description: Established to build India's first supercomputer after US denial.

actors.name: Mahindra & Mahindra Limited
actors.description: Major manufacturer of utility vehicles and tractors.

events.description: Produced first utility vehicle for the Indian market.

actors.name: Flipkart
actors.description: Leading e-commerce platform that pioneered online retail in India.

events.description: Founded by Sachin Bansal and Binny Bansal.
Launched 'Cash on Delivery' model, adapting tech to Indian market.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi (IITD)
actors.description: Premier engineering institute with strong ties to policy.

events.description: Declared an Institution of National Importance.

actors.name: Tata Motors
actors.description: Largest Indian automobile manufacturer by revenue.

events.description: Founded as Tata Engineering and Locomotive Co. Ltd.
Acquired Jaguar Land Rover from Ford.
Tested Altroz EV, pushing electric vehicle transition.

actors.name: National Thermal Power Corporation (NTPC)
actors.description: India's largest power utility company.

actors.name: Digital India Corporation (DIC)
actors.description: Implementing agency for the Digital India programme.

events.description: Launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

actors.name: Serum Institute of India (SII)
actors.description: World's largest vaccine manufacturer by number of doses produced.

events.description: Supplied Covishield vaccine for India's COVID-19 vaccination drive.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur (IITK)
actors.description: Institute known for aeronautical engineering and computer science.

events.description: Established with assistance from a consortium of US universities.
Started the first Computer Science education program in India.

actors.name: Tech Mahindra
actors.description: Provider of IT and networking technology solutions.

actors.name: National Dairy Development Board (NDDB)
actors.description: Promoted Operation Flood and created AMUL model.

events.description: Established under an Act of Parliament.
Launched 'Operation Flood', the world's largest dairy development program.

actors.name: Bharat Biotech
actors.description: Leading biotechnology company focused on vaccine innovation.

events.description: Developed India's first indigenous COVID-19 vaccine, Covaxin.

actors.name: Antrix Corporation
actors.description: Commercial arm of ISRO.

events.description: Launched 5 UK satellites on a single PSLV rocket.

actors.name: Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) - Swadeshi Jagran Manch
actors.description: Advocates for swadeshi (indigenous) technology and economic policies.

actors.name: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL)
actors.description: Major oil and natural gas company.

events.description: Formed by merger of Esso and Caltex.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur (IITKGP)
actors.description: First IIT, established to train engineers for nation-building.

events.description: Inaugurated in the Hijli Detention Camp.

actors.name: Bajaj Auto Limited
actors.description: World's largest three-wheeler manufacturer.

actors.name: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited
actors.description: Largest pharma company in India by market capitalization.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee (IITR)
actors.description: Oldest technical institution in Asia.

events.description: Founded as College of Civil Engineering.

actors.name: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
actors.description: Executive agency for IT policy and electronics manufacturing.

events.description: Carved out of the Department of Electronics and IT.

actors.name: Coal India Limited (CIL)

events.description: Nationalized and formed as Coal India Limited.

actors.name: Pharmaceuticals Export Promotion Council of India (Pharmexcil)
actors.description: Promotes pharma exports and technology transfer.

events.description: Established by the Ministry of Commerce.

actors.name: RedBus (Ibibo Group)
actors.description: Pioneer in online bus ticketing aggregation.

actors.name: Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL)
actors.description: Manufacturer of guided missile systems.

actors.name: Larsen & Toubro Infotech (LTI)

events.description: Spun off from L&T's IT division.

actors.name: MakeMyTrip
actors.description: Leading online travel company.

actors.name: Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI)

events.description: Developed high-yielding wheat varieties instrumental in Green Revolution.

actors.name: Intel India
actors.description: Largest non-manufacturing R&D center for Intel outside US.

events.description: Opened new silicon design center in Bangalore.

actors.name: Tata Steel
actors.description: Asia's first private steel company.

events.description: Commissioned India's first plant to extract methane from coal mines.

actors.name: Microland
actors.description: Pioneer in remote infrastructure management services.

actors.name: IIT Madras Research Park
actors.description: First of its kind research park in India promoting industry-academia collaboration.

actors.name: Godrej & Boyce Manufacturing Company
actors.description: Diversified engineering conglomerate.

actors.name: Nasscom (National Association of Software and Service Companies)
actors.description: Apex body for the IT-BPM industry.

events.description: Played key role in liberalizing telecom policy.

actors.name: Zomato

events.description: Listed on stock exchanges with massive IPO.

actors.name: NewSpace Research and Technologies
actors.description: Private player in aerospace and defense R&D.

actors.description: Manages technical manpower for Indian Railways.

actors.name: Amul (Gujarat Co-operative Milk Marketing Federation)
actors.description: Pioneered the cooperative model for dairy technology distribution.

events.description: Created the 'Amul Girl' campaign, innovating rural marketing.

actors.description: Offline UPI QR code payment provider for merchants.

actors.name: NITI Aayog

actors.name: Bajaj Finserv
actors.description: Pioneer in EMI financing on consumer goods.

events.description: First passenger train ran between Bombay and Thane.
Launched 'Train 18' (Vande Bharat), India's first engineless train.

actors.name: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories
actors.description: First Indian pharma company to be listed on NYSE.

actors.name: AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education)
actors.description: Statutory body for planning and coordinating technical education.

events.description: Constituted as a statutory body via AICTE Act.

actors.name: ISRO Telemetry, Tracking and Command Network (ISTRAC)
actors.description: Provides mission support for launch vehicles and satellites.

actors.description: Leading fashion e-commerce platform.

actors.name: Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati (IITG)

actors.description: Online food ordering and delivery platform.

actors.description: Develops radiation-processed agricultural products.

actors.description: Leading digital payments and financial services company.

events.description: Founded by Vijay Shekhar Sharma as One97 Communications.

events.description: Acquired Novelis, becoming the world's largest aluminium rolling company.

actors.name: Hero MotoCorp
actors.description: World's largest two-wheeler manufacturer.

events.description: Joint venture formed as Hero Honda.
Split from Honda; rebranded as Hero MotoCorp.

actors.description: Top new IIT with focus on AI and data science.

events.description: Founded by Tarun Mehta and Swapnil Jain.
Launched Ather 450, India's first smart electric scooter.

actors.name: Software Technology Parks of India (STPI)
actors.description: Provides infrastructure and incentives for software exports.

actors.name: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Missile Complex
actors.description: Cluster of DRDO labs for missile development.

actors.name: PhonePe

actors.name: Ashok Leyland

actors.name: Central Electronics Limited (CEL)
